summary: chai-check-error typosquats chaijs/check-error and executes remote code on import
details: Package name and metadata (author 'Jake Luer', repository git+ssh://git@github.com/chaijs/check-error.git) impersonate the legitimate chaijs `check-error` utility. On require()/import, index.js calls _initMsgCache() at module top level: deriveSeed() builds a hex string from a hardcoded 159-byte table via an LCG-seeded sbox combined with XOR/subtract/bit-rotate operations, the suffix is hex-decoded into an AES-256-CBC key/iv/ciphertext, the plaintext URL is fetched over HTTPS, and the JSON response's `cookie` field is executed via `new Function('require', mod)(require)` — granting attacker-controlled JavaScript full Node `require` access. The cover-story name `_msgCache` is unrelated to the public API (a getMessage utility that mirrors the real check-error exports). Unrelated runtime dependencies (axios, form-data, socket.io-client) further diverge from the utility's purported purpose and broaden post-exploitation capability. Loading this package — directly or transitively through any chai consumer — yields full remote code execution on the installer/runtime host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
